name: Horse Racing
description: Triple Crown, Royal Ascot, the Grand National — and the most famous racehorses ever.

questions.q: Which race is known as 'The Most Exciting Two Minutes in Sports'?
What three races make up the U.S. Triple Crown?

source.notes: Run in this order each spring: KY Derby (1st Sat in May) → Preakness (3 wks later) → Belmont (3 wks later).

questions.q: Which Kentucky racetrack hosts the Kentucky Derby?
Which Maryland racetrack hosts the Preakness Stakes?
Which 1973 horse won the Triple Crown by 31 lengths in the Belmont Stakes?

source.notes: Set still-standing track records in all three Triple Crown races.

questions.q: Which 2015 horse ended a 37-year drought to win the U.S. Triple Crown?

source.notes: Trained by Bob Baffert; ridden by Victor Espinoza.

questions.q: Which 2018 horse won the Triple Crown undefeated in his entire racing career?

source.notes: Six races, six wins. Trained by Bob Baffert.

questions.q: Which is the OLDEST of the three U.S. Triple Crown races?
Which is the LONGEST of the three U.S. Triple Crown races?

source.notes: Belmont is the longest at 1½ miles ('The Test of the Champion').

questions.q: Which prestigious British race is held over four days at Royal Ascot in June?

source.notes: The Queen Anne Stakes opens the meeting.

questions.q: Which famous British steeplechase has a 4-mile, 30-fence course at Aintree?
Which iconic Italian-born jockey rode 7 winners on a single Champions Day at Ascot in 1996?

source.notes: His 'Magnificent Seven' is one of the greatest feats in horse racing.

questions.q: Who is the most prolific U.S. jockey of all time, with over 9,000 career wins?

source.notes: Russell Baze retired in 2016 with 12,842 wins — most in North American history.

questions.q: What hat is traditionally worn at the Kentucky Derby?

source.notes: Founder Col. M. Lewis Clark introduced European-style fashion as part of the Derby's culture.

questions.q: What is the official drink of the Kentucky Derby?

source.notes: Bourbon, mint, sugar, ice. About 120,000 are served Derby weekend.

questions.q: What is the name of the song traditionally played at the Kentucky Derby just before post time?

source.notes: Composed by Stephen Foster in 1853. Played by University of Louisville Marching Band.

questions.q: What are the three forms of horse racing?
choices: Flat racing, jump racing, harness racing

questions.q: What does it mean to win 'in a photo finish'?
choices: Won by less than a length
Won by less than a nose
Officials reviewed photo to determine winner

source.notes: Photo-finish cameras have been standard at major tracks since the 1930s.

questions.q: Which horse-racing term refers to an unscheduled or surprise winner at long odds?

source.notes: The phrase originated in 19th-century English horse racing.

questions.q: How many horses typically run in the Kentucky Derby?

source.notes: Limited to 20, the largest field in any major U.S. race.
